title: IDF Kills Brothers Carrying Water, Strikes Desalination Station in Shujayea — Gaza City
detail: Israeli forces killed brothers Mohammed and Eid Abu Warda as they transported water by vehicle on Mansoura Street in Shujayea neighborhood, east of Gaza City. A third brother was wounded with moderate injuries. Separately, an Israeli drone struck a water desalination station in the same Shujayea neighborhood, killing one civilian and injuring two others. UNICEF stated the Mansoura water filling station — the only operational truck filling point for the Mekorot water supply line serving Gaza City — was struck by Israeli fire early Friday morning. Two UNICEF-contracted water truck drivers (brothers Eid and Mahmoud Abu Warda per Xinhua) were killed and two others injured. UNICEF suspended all contractor activities at the site and called for an immediate Israeli investigation, warning that critical water infrastructure 'must never be targeted.' The attacks cut off water delivery to hundreds of thousands of Gazans dependent on the Mekorot line.

media.caption: UNICEF outraged: two water truck drivers killed by Israeli fire at Mansoura filling station, Gaza City

title: Israeli Settlers Torch Two Vehicles in Majd al-Ba'a, South Hebron — Arson from Otniel Settlement
detail: Armed Israeli settlers infiltrated from the illegal Otniel settlement into the Majd al-Ba'a area west of Yatta, south of Hebron, burning two vehicles belonging to Palestinian brothers Khaled and Yasser Abu Ali. Local activist Osama Makhmara confirmed the incident, which occurred on the same day as separate arson and vandalism attacks attributed to settlers at other West Bank villages. The incident is part of a sustained pattern of settler violence that the IDF's own data shows increased 27% in 2025 and is continuing to accelerate in 2026.

media.caption: Settler attack torches Palestinian vehicles in south Hebron area, West Bank

title: Hamas Reiterates Refusal to Disarm, Placing Gaza Ceasefire Phase 2 in Jeopardy
detail: Hamas political leadership publicly reiterated its refusal to disarm as a condition of the Gaza ceasefire's Phase 2 negotiations, declaring disarmament a 'red line' that the movement would not cross. The IDF separately reported 14 ceasefire violations by Palestinian factions between April 8-16 alone, bringing the total since October 2025 to 22+ violations. Hamas's stated position makes Phase 2 — which requires Hamas disarmament as a precondition for full Israeli military withdrawal — increasingly unworkable. The stance also complicates the Trump administration's Board of Peace reconstruction framework, which requires Hamas political disengagement from Gaza governance.

media.caption: Hamas reiterates refusal to disarm, placing Gaza ceasefire Phase 2 in jeopardy
